name: create_file
description: Create a new file with content, auto-creating parent directories. Supports literal text (single argument), reading from a source file (prefix with @), and reading from stdin (-). Avoids heredoc escaping issues in shell.
when_to_use: Use when you need to create a new source file or config file. Replaces 2-3 attempted heredoc commands that often fail due to escaping issues. Supports multiple content sources: literal string, file reference, or stdin pipe. Use @filepath to copy an existing file, or use quoted text for inline content.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the new file (e.g., evaluator/modules.go). Parent directories are created automatically.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Content to write. Can be literal text (use quotes), @/path/to/src (copy file), or - (read from stdin). Default: empty file.

examples.expected: Creates evaluator/modules.go with the provided content, auto-creating evaluator/ directory.
Copies template file to the new location, auto-creating parent directories.

cost_saving_rationale: Eliminates trial-and-error with heredoc escaping in shell (single quotes, variable interpolation, indentation issues). Combines mkdir -p and file creation into one step, reducing a common failure point. The @file feature avoids retyping or copying content manually.
